programmes.show_description: A Tory conference gets a pasting in the rapid-fire news quiz, and Milton Jones, Seann Walsh, Miles Jupp and Josh Widdicombe join the regulars to suggest 'Unlikely Small Ads'!
Panel show giggles. Jimmy Carr, Griff Rhys Jones, Dave Myers and Susanna Reid join Rob Brydon, David Mitchell and Lee Mack to discern unlikely fact from plausible fiction.
Unseen Bits: Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell return with an episode of previously unseen material from the fourteenth series of the show.
Making up Richard Ayoade's self-quizzing collective this time are Dane Baptiste, Angela Barnes, and Lou Sanders.
Monster Mash: The smash-hit quiz show that caught on in a flash. Beastly quizmaster Stephen Fry is an eerie sight for Sara Pascoe, Josh Widdicombe, Phill Jupitus and Alan Davies.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Pub Grub: The bikers head to the pub to explore the story behind the boom in pub food and snacks and recreate mouthwatering dishes that would make any publican proud.
A man kidnapped by a Mexican drug cartel flees across the bridge into America. At Progreso International Bridge, a woman is caught hiding anti-anxiety pills.
A refugee from Venezuela tells officers how she paid human traffickers to smuggle her to the Mexico-US border. In Laredo, an American couple are caught smuggling methamphetamine.
Documentary series. One man and a huge train take on the unforgiving Pilbara, and four extreme train enthusiasts in Sydney tackle its rail network in the All Stations Challenge.
Bangladesh: Rick Stein ends his epic culinary tour in Bangladesh and, not surprisingly, curry is at the top of his list of local delicacies.
A truck with a shipment of wet wipes turns out to have a secret hidden inside, and a US citizen is caught with thousands of Tramadol tablets hidden in his hand luggage.
A huge shipment of cocaine is found hidden in the floor of a truck, a young man tries to enter the US to enrol in school, and a woman is caught smuggling fentanyl pills.
Mountain Rescue Dog Handler: Guy loves dogs - he therefore jumps at the opportunity to become part of a dog mountain rescue team tackling dangerous terrain as a mountain rescue guide.
Jeremy Clarkson subjects the Skoda Yeti to a thorough road test, while James May visits the test track in the insane Aerial Atom V8.
The lads go head-to-head against their opposite numbers from the Australian Top Gear in a car-based version of The Ashes - howzat sound? Tennis star Boris Becker also guests.
Pub Grub: The bikers head to the pub to explore the story behind the boom in pub food and snacks and recreate mouthwatering dishes that would make any publican proud.
Bangladesh: Rick Stein ends his epic culinary tour in Bangladesh and, not surprisingly, curry is at the top of his list of local delicacies.
Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell sort fact from fiction with guests John Cooper Clarke, Judi Love, Chris McCausland and Caroline Quentin.
Retired footballer David James, comedians Rhys James and Isy Suttie, and athletics legend Denise Lewis tackle the best-selling author's fun-filled brainteasers in this edition.
More mind-bending puzzles and fun with the ever-genial Pointless personality - he's joined this time by Sara Barron, Anton Du Beke, Jessica Fostekew and Rickie Haywood-Williams.
Messy: Quite complex quizzing. Things get messy as Stephen Fry learns whether Sarah Millican, Eddie Kadi, Noel Fielding and Alan Davies know their mugwumps from their mullipuffs.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: Alexander Armstrong is host as comedians Jo Brand and Munya Chawawa join team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop to delve into the news.
Bready Bready Bready: Jo Brand demonstrates an impressive grasp of street slang, Katy Wix uses a long part of her body to steal something, and David Baddiel takes a sledgehammer to a problem.
Missed the mockers? Have no fear, as Dara O Briain rounds up the wittiest slices of satire from Series 12 of the superb news quiz - with some outrageous outtakes thrown in too.
Compilation: Rob Brydon returns with a special episode of the rib-tickling, fib-tackling comedy panel show, featuring all the best outtakes and unseen bits from series 7.
Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell sort fact from fiction with guests John Cooper Clarke, Judi Love, Chris McCausland and Caroline Quentin.
Messy: Quite complex quizzing. Things get messy as Stephen Fry learns whether Sarah Millican, Eddie Kadi, Noel Fielding and Alan Davies know their mugwumps from their mullipuffs.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: Alexander Armstrong is host as comedians Jo Brand and Munya Chawawa join team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop to delve into the news.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Preserving: The bikers journey through time, celebrating British food. Here they explore how some of our tastiest treats have evolved from a need to preserve.
Officers intercept an arsenal of weapons being smuggled into Mexico. A consignment of Fentanyl is discovered in a freezer.
Officers discover the fuel tanks on a truck are full of liquid meth. Three men wearing identical clothing cause suspicion. A man is caught smuggling reptiles.
Documentary series. Bernie puts the first-ever Streamliners festival together, but unprecedented levels of flooding in New South Wales threaten to end his dream.
Corsica and Sardinia: Join Rick Stein on a culinary tour of the sun-kissed Mediterranean. In Sardinia, Rick samples an especially good spaghetti with cuttlefish ink and tomato sauce.
A woman is caught trying to enter the US with a baby that isn't hers, a man wanted for a serious crime is intercepted, and a driver has a heart attack while crossing a bridge.
Methamphetamine is found in a shipment of broccoli, an elderly man tries to smuggle pills in protein powder, and a woman receives a humanitarian visa to see her dying son.
Lifeboat Crew: Guy Martin joins the crew of an RNLI lifeboat in his hometown of Grimsby as he tackles a selfless and demanding job where volunteers put their lives on the line every day.
Buckle up for more motoring japes. The team test luxurious cars including the Rolls-Royce Ghost, the Bentley Mulsanne and the Mercedes S65 AMG in Albania, and Jonathan Ross guests.
The motor-mad motormouths discover some of the odd pitfalls of picking up a used car at a low price. Star Trek's Simon Pegg is beamed up to the Reasonably Priced Car.
Preserving: The bikers journey through time, celebrating British food. Here they explore how some of our tastiest treats have evolved from a need to preserve.
Corsica and Sardinia: Join Rick Stein on a culinary tour of the sun-kissed Mediterranean. In Sardinia, Rick samples an especially good spaghetti with cuttlefish ink and tomato sauce.
Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell sort more fact from fiction with guests Angellica Bell, Bob Mortimer, Suggs and Holly Willoughby.
Retired footballer David James, comedians Rhys James and Isy Suttie, and athletics legend Denise Lewis tackle the best-selling author's fun-filled brainteasers in this edition.
More mind-bending puzzles and fun with the ever-genial Pointless personality - he's joined this time by Sara Barron, Anton Du Beke, Jessica Fostekew and Rickie Haywood-Williams.
Misconceptions: The witty quiz that thrives on fallacies. Sue Perkins, Sara Cox and Chris Addison guest as departing host Stephen Fry docks Alan Davies points one last time.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: Martin Lewis is host as comedian Ignacio Lopez and broadcaster Steph McGovern join team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop to delve into the news.
A Cuddle: The competitors are lured to a wasteland for a challenge involving eggs and medical equipment - David Baddiel spray-paints his face and Jo Brand reveals her psychic superpowers.
